era_context: Middle Bronze Age; Hyksos rule in Egypt; Old Babylonian period in Mesopotamia; Shang dynasty in China; Middle Minoan Crete. This is a period of regional consolidation and trade network development. Large empires are fragmenting, and smaller kingdoms are asserting power.

events.title: Samsu-iluna's reign in Babylon — administrative consolidation
events.description: Samsu-iluna continues his reign in Babylon, managing the decline of Old Babylonian power. By this period, Kassite pressure from the east and Amorite incursions from the west were fragmenting Babylonian control. Samsu-iluna's reign is attested in cuneiform documents and king lists, though no specific event is recorded for -1769 BCE.
events.certainty_note: Samsu-iluna's reign is well-attested, but no specific event is documented for -1769 BCE. This entry reflects the broader context of his reign.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: This year is not specifically documented in surviving sources. The historical record for -1769 BCE relies entirely on regnal lists and archaeological context. No narrative sources describe events of this year. The assignment of Samsu-iluna's reign to this period is based on king list chronologies and radiocarbon calibration, both of which carry significant uncertainty (±10–20 years).
